name: Russell Dickerson
bio: Modern country-pop romantic. Writes almost exclusively about his wife Kailey and their life together. Slyly incorporates modern R&B rhythms into bright, tuneful mainstream country-pop. Recent Famous Back Home (2025) era infuses country-soul foundation with lush synth and 80s rock texture. Casey Brown is his signature producer.
producer_notes: Casey Brown production — clean, bright, warm Nashville sheen. Acoustic guitar forward over modern production. R&B vocal phrasing over country instrumentation.

songs.title: What a Life
songs.dna: Uptempo country-pop with warm acoustic guitar, lush modern production. Pure gratitude and wonder at a life better than he ever imagined. Wide open chorus, vocal harmonies.
songs.style_prompt: Warm country-pop, bright acoustic guitar lead, lush modern production, punchy drum machine, male tenor vocal open and joyful, pedal steel accents, layered backing harmonies on chorus, 130 BPM, arms-wide-open anthem energy, clean polished mix with organic country warmth, celebratory gratitude tone, wide dynamic chorus, R&B-influenced vocal runs, feel-good and unguarded throughout
songs.lyric_prompt: Write a joyful, upbeat country-pop anthem from a man who can barely believe how good his life has turned out — his wife, his kids, his friendships, the simple moments that make ordinary days feel extraordinary. The tone is genuine, warm disbelief: not bragging, just grateful shock. He catalogues specific small moments. The chorus should be a wide-open declaration. No irony, no darkness. Verses are conversational storytelling, chorus is anthemic singalong. Under 3:30.

songs.title: Yours
songs.dna: 134 BPM, B major. Warm acoustic guitar-driven with soaring chorus. Written about his wife Kailey. The Knot called it everything a first dance song should be. About a man who was lost before he found her.
songs.style_prompt: Warm devotion country-pop, 134 BPM, acoustic guitar-driven with soaring electric accents, pedal steel warmth, male tenor vocal earnest and full-hearted, sweeping emotional chorus with layered harmonies, polished Nashville production with organic instruments, first-dance energy but not slow — upbeat heartfelt, clean bright mix, deeply romantic without being sappy, declarative chorus hook, R&B vocal phrasing over country instrumentation
songs.lyric_prompt: Write a deeply romantic country-pop song from a man declaring complete, joyful belonging to the woman he loves. Before her, he was directionless and lost — she gave him a reason, an anchor, a home. The chorus is a pure declaration of devotion. Avoid clichés — replace every generic love lyric with a specific image. This is a first-dance song that also works as an anthem. Under 3:40.

songs.title: 16 Me
songs.dna: Reflects on growing up and looking back. Famous Back Home album theme: dreaming of being famous everywhere, then realizing being famous back home with your family is the real dream. Filtered through the lens of a man who now has a wife, kids, and a full life.
songs.style_prompt: Warm nostalgic country-pop with modern muscle, 95 BPM, acoustic guitar forward with subtle lush synth bed underneath, pedal steel warmth, 80s-influenced production touches, male tenor vocal sincere and reflective — looking back as a content man, layered harmonies building on chorus, clean polished Nashville mix, small-town growing-up imagery, bittersweet-hopeful nostalgic tone
songs.lyric_prompt: Write a warm nostalgic country-pop song structured as a letter from a man to his 16-year-old self. He speaks from genuine, specific contentment: a wife he loves completely, kids who run to the door when he gets home. He tells his younger self what's coming — to reassure him that the small-town dreams were never wrong, just the destination was different. He thought famous meant big stages — turns out it means being famous in his own home. Under 3:30.

songs.title: Happen to Me
songs.dna: Features palm-muted guitars. A Cyndi Lauper co-writer contributed. Arena-ready explosiveness. Fast, energetic, danceable. The central emotion: amazement that this woman chose him.
songs.style_prompt: Uptempo arena country-pop, 168 BPM, palm-muted electric guitar driving rhythm, punchy snare, danceable modern country-pop-rock hybrid, male vocal energetic and charismatic, bright production with 80s pop-rock influence, singalong anthemic chorus with layered harmonies, high-energy throughout — no slow moments, big stadium feel within country framework
songs.lyric_prompt: Write a fast, danceable country-pop anthem from a man who is genuinely amazed that the most incredible woman he's ever seen chose him. Joyful, slightly disbelieving gratitude. The chorus is a big, singalong explosion about how she happened to him — like a force of nature. Verses are energetic and narrative: how they met, the specific moment he realized he was done for. Under 3:20.

songs.title: Sorry
songs.vibe: Romantic apology · co-written with Charles Kelley
songs.dna: Co-written by Charles Kelley, Casey Brown, and Russell Dickerson. Key of B Major, 120 BPM. Genuine warm apology to his wife, delivered with earnest warmth. Not dramatic — just honest.
songs.style_prompt: Warm mid-tempo country-pop apology ballad, 120 BPM, acoustic guitar-forward, pedal steel tenderness, male tenor vocal sincere and gentle — not dramatic, just honest, soft drum groove, clean Nashville production, emotionally open without being overwrought, layered harmonies adding warmth on chorus, intimate scale
songs.lyric_prompt: Write a genuine, warm country-pop apology song from a man who knows he messed up — not dramatically, but in the small consistent ways that wear on a relationship: being distracted, taking her for granted, putting other things first. He's not groveling — he's just being honest. The chorus is a direct, specific apology that names what he did wrong. Under 3:30.

songs.title: Home Sweet
songs.dna: B Major, 121 BPM. Core idea: home isn't a place, it's her. Warm and domestic, celebrating the simple life together.
songs.style_prompt: Warm domestic country-pop, 121 BPM, bright acoustic guitar, pedal steel warmth, male vocal tender and content, mid-tempo feel-good groove, layered harmonies on chorus, clean Nashville production, simple-life celebration energy, porch swing Sunday atmosphere, love-is-home thematic core, no ambition — just gratitude for the ordinary
songs.lyric_prompt: Write a warm, domestic country-pop song built on the idea that home is not a place — it's a person. A man who has traveled, struggled financially, moved around, and through it all found that wherever she is, that's home. Contrast moments of not-quite-having-it-together with the feeling of total contentment when they're together. Under 3:30.

songs.title: God Gave Me a Girl
songs.dna: 142 BPM, G major. Inspired by the birth of his child and seeing his wife Kailey sing to her. About becoming a father and the overwhelming, immediate, permanent love for a daughter.
songs.style_prompt: Uplifting high-energy country-pop, 142 BPM, bright electric and acoustic guitar blend, big drum production, male vocal full and reverent — more powerful than his usual tender mode, soaring chorus with wide harmonies, production has extra lift and grandeur, G major bright and open, fatherhood-devotion emotional core, overwhelmingly warm
songs.lyric_prompt: Write an uplifting country-pop song from a new father to his daughter. He didn't fully understand what he was capable of feeling until the moment she arrived. The tone is reverent and overwhelmed with love — genuinely awestruck. The chorus is a declaration of fatherly devotion using the 'God gave me' concept — the idea that she is a gift he didn't earn. Include a verse about watching her sleep, or her small hands, or hearing her laugh for the first time. Under 3:15.

songs.title: MGNO (My Girl's Night Out)
songs.vibe: Playful devotion · loving her freedom from a distance
songs.dna: MGNO = My Girl's Night Out. A man who is home alone while his wife/girlfriend is out with her friends, and rather than insecurity or jealousy, he feels pure adoration watching her be free and happy. Secure in love.
songs.style_prompt: Warm playful country-pop, 125 BPM, bright acoustic guitar lead, pedal steel accents, punchy mid-tempo drum groove, male vocal charming and utterly secure — no jealousy, just adoration, layered harmonies on chorus, clean Casey Brown-style Nashville production, slightly humorous but genuinely loving tone, feels like a man happily home alone knowing she'll come back
songs.lyric_prompt: Write a playful, warm country-pop song from a man whose girlfriend or wife is out with her friends for the night — and rather than feeling jealous or left out, he is completely, genuinely happy for her. The tone is secure, charming, a little self-aware about how besotted he is. The chorus is a celebration of her night out — he wants her to have the best time, knowing she's coming home to him. Include a verse about imagining what she and her friends are doing right now, delivered with warmth and humor. The bridge is the moment she texts him or comes home. Under 3:15.
